reactant_pattern_text: aldehyde or two aldehydes under catalyst
common_conditions: metal alkoxide or Lewis acidic catalyst, often aldehyde dimerization or crossed coupling
description_short: Hydride-transfer coupling of aldehydes to esters or 1,3-diol monoesters under metal alkoxide or related catalysis.

mechanism_type: oxidative addition to allyl precursor followed by nucleophilic substitution
common_conditions: Pd catalyst, often phosphine ligand, soft or hard nucleophile control
description_short: Palladium-catalyzed allylic substitution proceeding through π-allylpalladium intermediates with rich nucleophile-dependent regio- and stereocontrol.

transformation_type: deformylation / decarbonylation of aldehydes or acyl derivatives
mechanism_type: Rh-mediated oxidative addition and CO extrusion
reactant_pattern_text: aldehyde or acyl halide + Wilkinson catalyst
common_conditions: Wilkinson catalyst at room temperature to reflux
description_short: Rhodium-catalyzed removal of carbon monoxide from aldehydes and acyl derivatives to furnish decarbonylated products.

transformation_type: four-component assembly of α-acylamino amides and variants
mechanism_type: imine formation / isocyanide capture / acyl transfer
reactant_pattern_text: amine + carbonyl + isocyanide + acid or related nucleophile
common_conditions: mild multicomponent condensation often at 0 °C to room temperature
description_short: Four-component condensation of amines, carbonyl compounds, isocyanides, and acidic components to access peptide-like adducts and heterocycles.

family_name: Ullmann Biaryl Ether and Biaryl Amine Synthesis / Condensation
common_conditions: copper salts with base, often elevated temperature
synonym_names: Ullmann condensation|Ullmann biaryl ether synthesis|Ullmann biaryl amine synthesis
description_short: Copper-mediated coupling of aryl halides with phenols or amines to form biaryl ethers and diarylamines.
